skill.name: deployment-procedures
skill.description: Guides safe production deployments with platform selection, readiness checks, rollback planning, and post-release verification.
skill.summary: Plan safer production deployments with clear checks, rollback criteria, and verification windows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static command findings at SKILL.md lines 28, 43, and 85 are Markdown code fence markers and deployment diagrams, not executable Ruby or shell backticks. The weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 is also a false positive because the line is plain description text. The skill does declare Bash as an allowed tool, so publication is acceptable with a low operational risk note.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fences Misidentified as Command Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged Ruby or shell backtick execution at SKILL.md lines 28, 43, and 85. These lines are Markdown code fence delimiters around diagrams and process steps, not executable commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The exact lines contain Markdown fence syntax and surrounding text is instructional. No shell invocation, Ruby interpolation, or user-controlled execution path is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Plain Text
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged a weak cryptographic algorithm at SKILL.md line 3. The line is frontmatter description text about deployment procedures, and no cryptographic algorithm or operation appears there.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited line contains only a natural-language description. I did not find evidence of hashing, encryption, signing, or weak crypto use at that location.

low_findings.title: Bash Tool Permission Requires Deployment Context
low_findings.description: The skill declares Bash in allowed tools at SKILL.md line 4. The content teaches principles and does not provide scripts, but Bash access can run external commands when a user requests deployment actions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission is explicit in frontmatter, so external command capability exists. Risk is limited because the skill text discourages script copying and contains no executable deployment automation.

content.value_statement: Production releases fail when teams skip readiness checks, backups, and rollback planning. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structure safer deployment decisions.
actual_capabilities: Explains deployment platform choices for static sites, web apps, microservices, serverless systems, and Kubernetes.
Provides pre-deployment checks for tests, builds, environment variables, backups, rollback plans, and monitoring.
Organizes releases into prepare, backup, deploy, verify, and confirm or rollback phases.
Defines verification windows for immediate monitoring, short-term stability checks, and next-day review.
Compares rollback methods for Vercel, Netlify, Railway, Render, VPS, Docker, and Kubernetes.
Describes rolling, blue-green, and canary strategies for reducing downtime risk.
limitations: Does not provide copy-ready deployment scripts or platform-specific command sequences.
Does not validate live infrastructure state, credentials, cloud accounts, or monitoring dashboards.
Requires users to adapt guidance to their stack, hosting provider, compliance needs, and incident process.
Cannot replace experienced release approval for high-risk migrations or regulated production systems.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Small Web App Release
use_cases.description: Build a release checklist that covers tests, build output, environment variables, backups, rollback steps, and monitoring ownership.

use_cases.title: Review a Production Change Plan
use_cases.description: Evaluate whether a planned release has clear deployment phases, rollback triggers, verification checks, and team communication.

use_cases.title: Choose a Safer Rollout Strategy
use_cases.description: Compare rolling, blue-green, and canary deployment options based on release risk, rollback speed, and validation needs.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Deployment Checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the deployment-procedures skill to create a production deployment checklist for my app. Include readiness, backup, deploy, verify, and rollback items.

prompt_templates.title: Adapt Steps to My Platform
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the deployment-procedures skill to adapt a safe deployment workflow for [platform]. Focus on verification, monitoring, and rollback decisions.

prompt_templates.title: Assess Rollback Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the deployment-procedures skill to review my rollback plan. Identify missing triggers, backup needs, communication steps, and verification checks.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Low-Risk Rollout Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the deployment-procedures skill to compare rolling, blue-green, and canary deployment for this change. Recommend a strategy with rollback criteria.

output_examples.input: Plan a production deployment for a small Node web app on Render.
output: A phase-based checklist covering readiness, backup, deployment, verification, and rollback.
A monitoring window with immediate, fifteen-minute, one-hour, and next-day checks.
Rollback criteria for downtime, critical errors, and severe performance degradation.

output_examples.input: Help decide between canary and blue-green for a risky API change.
output: A comparison of rollback speed, validation quality, operational complexity, and traffic control.
A recommendation based on blast radius, dependency risk, and monitoring confidence.
A short list of release gates and stop conditions.

output_examples.input: Review whether my team is ready to deploy today.
output: A readiness assessment for tests, builds, secrets, migrations, backups, and team coverage.
A list of missing information that should block or delay release.
A concise go or no-go recommendation based on deployment risk.

best_practices: Define rollback triggers before deployment starts, not after symptoms appear.
Keep monitoring open during deployment and verify core user flows immediately after release.
Use smaller releases, feature flags, and staged rollouts when changes affect production users.
anti_patterns: Deploying without a tested backup or documented rollback path.
Combining many unrelated production changes into one release.
Leaving immediately after deployment without checking health, logs, performance, and key flows.

faq.question: Does this skill run deployments for me?
faq.answer: No. It teaches deployment decision-making and checklist structure. Users must execute platform-specific steps in their own environment.

faq.question: Which platforms does it cover?
faq.answer: It discusses Vercel, Netlify, Railway, Render, VPS, Docker, Kubernetes, serverless, and similar deployment models.

faq.question: Can it help with rollback planning?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains rollback triggers, backup readiness, platform rollback options, and post-incident review.

faq.question: Is it suitable for regulated production systems?
faq.answer: It can support planning, but regulated systems still need formal approvals, audit controls, and organization-specific procedures.

faq.question: Does it include zero-downtime guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains rolling, blue-green, and canary strategies and when each approach is useful.

faq.question: Why is the security risk low?
faq.answer: The skill allows Bash, but the content is instructional and contains no scripts, network calls, secret access, or prompt injection.
